title: Additional costs of car ownership
description: Practice understanding of additional costs of car ownership, from determining what all goes into additional costs, to how distinguishing among different cost levels associated with different cars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Owning a car isn’t just about the monthly payment. There are costs you absolutely must cover, and others that are optional.

**Which of these are costs you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Owning a car isn’t just about the monthly payment. There are costs you absolutely must cover, and others that are optional.

**Which of these are costs you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Owning a car isn’t just about the monthly payment. There are costs you absolutely must cover, and others that are optional.

**Which of these are costs you have to pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're about to buy a new, mid-size SUV to replace your current older sedan. The SUV has a reputation for being safe and comes with the latest technology. 

**Considering this change, what will likely happen to your insurance costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: they will likely stay the same

assessment_items.question_content:
Because gas is getting expensive, you're buying a new sedan. It will replace your new, large truck that uses a lot of gas.

**Considering this change, what will likely happen to your insurance costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You decide to switch from a 2-row to a 3-row SUV of the same year, make and model. 

**Considering this change, what will likely happen to your insurance costs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

For people who drive a lot, the cost of gas can really add up. In some cases, it can be as high as their monthly car payment.

**Knowing this, which car would use the *least* amount of gas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are looking to purchase a specific sedan. The manufacturer offers 4, 6 and 8-cylinder engines available with this model. 

**How does the 4-cylinder engine compare to the 8-cylinder engine?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: uses the same amount of fuel

assessment_items.question_content:
The vehicle you are looking at is offered in both gas and electric versions. 

**Which version of the vehicle will have the lowest cost to refuel or recharge?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The cost of car repairs might not catch your attention right away, but over time, it can significantly impact your budget.

**Complete the following sentence to accurately reflect repair costs.**

Cars with many features and electronic components typically have [[☃ dropdown 1]]  repair costs, compared to basic-model cars.
hints: Typically, more complexity means more work.
Features and electronic gadgets *add* to a car's complexity.
Working on cars that are complex takes advanced knowledge and a longer time to complete, both of which *increase* costs.
Car with many features and electronic components typically have **higher** repair costs , compared to basic-model cars.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cost of car repairs might not catch your attention right away, but over time, it can significantly impact your budget.

**Complete the following sentence to accurately reflect repair costs.**

Basic-model cars typically have [[☃ dropdown 1]]  repair costs, compared to those with many features and electronic components.
hints: Typically, more complexity means more work.
Features and electronic gadgets *add* to a car's complexity.
Working on cars that are more basic takes less knowledge and a shorter time to complete, both of which *decrease* costs.
Basic-model cars typically have **lower** repair costs, compared to to those with many features and electronic components.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cost of car repairs might not catch your attention right away, but over time, it can significantly impact your budget.

**Complete the following sentence to accurately reflect repair costs.**

Cars with  [[☃ dropdown 1]] additional features and electronic components have higher repair costs compared to basic-model cars.
hints: Typically, more complexity means more work.
Features and electronic gadgets *add* to a car's complexity.
Working on cars that are complex takes advanced knowledge and a longer time to complete, both of which increase costs.
Cars with *many* additional features and electronic components have higher repair costs compared to basic-model cars.
